title: The Holdovers
logline: A curmudgeonly classics teacher at a New England boarding school is forced to supervise students staying over Christmas break, forming an unlikely bond with a rebellious student and the school's head cook while confronting their shared loneliness and past wounds.
antagonistOrConflict: Institutional expectations vs. personal growth; loneliness and isolation vs. human connection
tone: Bittersweet, contemplative, with dry humor and warmth

actBreaks.description: End of setup - Paul and Angus are left alone after other students depart

validation.ruling: Scene #39 provides the true emotional end of Act 1 - the final departure of the other holdovers, leaving Angus completely alone. This moment of maximum isolation and Kountze's cruel return of the stolen photograph creates the perfect launching point into Act 2. Scene #36 is just the setup for departures, not the emotional payoff.

actBreaks.description: Winter break adventure ends, return to normal school life

validation.ruling: Scene #101 is the true end of Act 2 where Paul reveals his deepest secret - that he never graduated college and has been living a lie. This moment of complete vulnerability and truth-sharing between Paul and Angus represents the emotional climax of their relationship before external forces intervene in Act 3. The skating rink scene #98 is pleasant but lacks transformative power.

actBreaks.description: Confrontation with parents and headmaster about Boston trip consequences

validation.ruling: Scene #130 delivers the crisis that demands Paul's ultimate choice. The Clotfelters reveal the consequences of the Boston trip and threaten to send Angus to military school. This is where the stakes become clear and Paul must decide between self-preservation and protecting Angus. Scene #128 is just the setup - the real dramatic weight comes in #130.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: Character-driven three-act structure focused on relationship building during an extended second act that takes place over winter break, with a compressed third act dealing with consequences
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: Leisurely paced during the relationship-building middle section (scenes 36-98), with quick scene cuts and intimate moments. Final act accelerates dramatically with rapid consequences and resolution.

incitingIncident.description: Angus receives a devastating phone call from his mother Judy, who tells him last-minute that he must stay at school over break because she and her new husband Stanley are finally taking their honeymoon.

validation.ruling: Scene #19 is the true inciting incident where Angus receives the devastating phone call that his mother is abandoning him over Christmas break. This moment fundamentally changes his circumstances and sets the entire story in motion. Scene #20 is merely the procedural aftermath - Paul addressing the assembled holdovers. The call is the catalyst that forces Angus into the holdover situation that drives the narrative.

midpoint.description: At the Christmas party, Paul feels overwhelmed by the crowd while Miss Crane introduces him to her family members
midpoint.sceneHeading: INT. MISS CRANE’S HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - NIGHT

validation.ruling: Scene #71 perfectly captures the midpoint transformation. At Miss Crane's Christmas party, all three protagonists experience connection and belonging - Angus is enchanted by the snow globe memory and meets Elise, Paul mingles socially, and Mary finds community. This represents the emotional peak before consequences catch up, positioned ideally at 54.6%.

climax.description: Paul emerges from his meeting with Woodrup, who tells him he did this to himself. Paul responds by calling the headmaster 'penis cancer in human form,' showing he's sacrificed his career for Angus.

validation.ruling: Scene #132 delivers the climactic moment where Paul makes his ultimate sacrifice, delivering his devastating final insult to Dr. Woodrup that seals his fate but saves Angus. This is the culmination of Paul's character arc - choosing to protect his student over his own career security. At 95.4%, it's perfectly positioned for maximum dramatic impact.

scenes.heading: INT. CHAPEL - DAY
scenes.summary: Six boys in coat and tie hold hymnals by a Christmas tree, listening to their choirmaster who instructs them before they begin singing 'Oh Little Town of Bethlehem' beautifully.

scenes.heading: EXT. BARTON, MASS. - DAY
scenes.summary: Snow falls on the declining working-class New England town of Barton, Massachusetts, with taverns, churches, weather-beaten houses, and a shuttered textile mill by the river.

scenes.heading: EXT. BARTON ACADEMY - DAY
scenes.summary: Establishing shot of Barton Academy with its Gothic spires and Georgian buildings, presenting the ivy league romance of the prestigious school.

scenes.heading: INT. CHAPEL - DAY
scenes.summary: As the hymn concludes, the choirmaster smiles and critiques his young singers' performance, ending with praise: 'Very good. Excellent.'

scenes.heading: EXT. QUAD - DAY
scenes.summary: Boys hurry across the frigid campus quad, some tossing a football as they rush to class in the snowy weather.

scenes.heading: EXT. MAIN HALL - DAY
scenes.summary: Danny, a custodian, shovels the walkway trying to keep pace with the storm, stops to catch his breath and stares at the sky.

scenes.heading: EXT. FACULTY RESIDENCE - DAY
scenes.summary: Establishing shot of faculty residence with a single light shining in a top-floor window.

scenes.heading: INT. FACULTY RESIDENCE - PAUL’S ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: In his cramped, smoke-filled room crowded with books, classics teacher Paul Hunham grades exams while calling his students 'philistines.' He's interrupted by a woman's voice summoning him to see Dr. Woodrup.

scenes.heading: INT. FACULTY RESIDENCE - HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Miss Crane, a bright-eyed secretary, delivers Christmas cookies to Paul and informs him Dr. Woodrup wants to see him about Christmas break.

scenes.heading: INT. STUDENT DORMITORY - HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: 'Time Has Come Today' blares from a record player as boys in the dormitory hallway shout and shove while dressing in their school uniforms, preparing to go home.

scenes.heading: INT. STUDENT DORMITORY - DORM ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Seventeen-year-old Angus Tully hurriedly packs for St. Kitts, defending his black Speedo to mockery from Doug Crandall who calls it women's underwear.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Head cook Mary Lamb leads kitchen workers preparing breakfast over industrial ranges, glancing up to notice the snow falling outside the window.

scenes.heading: EXT. QUAD - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul crosses the snowy campus in his duffle coat with pipe between his teeth as boys race past him toward the dining hall.

scenes.heading: INT. DINING HALL - DAY
scenes.summary: In the grand dining hall, professors Rosenswieg and Endicott discuss who will be stuck supervising holdovers during Christmas break, glancing at Paul's empty chair.

scenes.heading: INT. HEADMASTER’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: In the headmaster's stately office, Dr. Woodrup thanks Paul for supervising the Christmas holdovers as an emergency favor, mentioning Mr. Endicott's mother's illness and offering Paul a bonus.

scenes.heading: INT. PAUL’S CLASSROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul bursts into his classroom and hands back final exams to ten impatient boys including Angus. He's particularly pleased about failing a student, while the boys react with dismay to their grades.

scenes.heading: INT. CHAPEL - DAY
scenes.summary: In the festive chapel service before Christmas break, Father Joe leads hymns while war memorial plaques honor fallen alumni including Curtis Ezra Lamb. Mary sits prominently in the front row.

scenes.heading: EXT. ADMINISTRATION BUILDING – DAY
scenes.summary: Boys rush toward luxury cars with luggage as Dr. Woodrup wishes everyone Merry Christmas. Angus scans the cars with growing concern before being called to take a phone call.

scenes.heading: INT. ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Angus receives a devastating phone call from his mother Judy, who tells him last-minute that he must stay at school over break because she and her new husband Stanley are finally taking their honeymoon.
scenes.turningPointNote: Angus's abandonment by his mother sets up the central emotional conflict

scenes.heading: INT. DORM COMMON ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul addresses five holdover boys in the dorm common room, including newly arrived red-eyed Angus. Paul attempts to maintain dignity about their situation while establishing the rules from the manual.
scenes.turningPointNote: The holdovers are officially assembled, beginning the central premise

scenes.heading: EXT. QUAD - DUSK
scenes.summary: Walking to the infirmary, the boys complain about being stuck with 'Walleye' Paul. Angus tries to stop Kountze from smoking marijuana openly, leading to tension between them.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - DUSK
scenes.summary: In the kitchen, Paul and Mary bond over drinks and cigarettes. Mary reveals she's staying because leaving feels like abandoning her dead son Curtis, while Paul admits he failed a student who deserved it.

scenes.heading: INT. INFIRMARY - DUSK
scenes.summary: In the infirmary dormitory, Angus accuses Kountze of stealing his photograph. Their confrontation escalates when Angus suggests Kountze's parents don't want him around either.

scenes.heading: INT. DINING HALL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: At dinner, Paul tells the boys about Romans eating sand during the siege of Carthage while they complain about the repetitive food. Kountze sits apart without a plate as punishment.

scenes.heading: INT. BARTON ACADEMY - VARIOUS LOCATIONS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Like a night watchman, Paul checks various campus locations with flashlight and keys, finally peering into the headmaster's office where his beam finds the bottle of cognac.

scenes.heading: INT. BACK STAIRWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Climbing stairs, Paul hears television laughter coming from above.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN STAFF COMMON ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Paul finds Mary in the kitchen staff common room watching 'The Newlywed Game' while smoking, shelling walnuts, and drinking bourbon. She invites him to sit and broaden his horizons.

scenes.heading: INT. INFIRMARY - MORNING
scenes.summary: Paul wakes the sleeping boys by banging bedpans and shouting 'It's daylight in the swamp' and 'Arise!' as they groan themselves awake.

scenes.heading: EXT. QUAD - DAY
scenes.summary: The boys run laps in the quad as Paul, smoking in his duffle coat, motions for them to go faster, shouting about exercise while coughing and hacking.

scenes.heading: INT. THE “SCHOOL ROOM” - DAY
scenes.summary: In the massive study hall, the boys study at desks while Paul reads at the proctor's table. Angus hides comics and a skin magazine inside his textbook, antagonizing the seething Kountze.

scenes.heading: INT. MARY’S APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: In her apartment filled with books and family photos of Curtis, Mary sits at her dining table working on a crossword puzzle with soft music playing.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVERBANK - DAY
scenes.summary: Walking by the river, the boys discuss escape plans. Smith reveals his CEO father has a helicopter and pilot named Wild Bill, sparking fantasies of rescue among the group.

scenes.heading: INT. INFIRMARY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Angus awakens to find Park crying from a nightmare and having wet his bed. Angus comforts him and helps hide the evidence, showing unexpected kindness to the younger boy.

scenes.heading: INT. FOUNDERS’ ROOM LIBRARY - DAY
scenes.summary: In the library, Paul drinks and coughs while the boys study. They hear a growing roar and rush to the window to see a helicopter about to land on campus.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Mary also hears the helicopter noise and approaches her kitchen window to observe the arrival.

scenes.heading: INT. FOUNDERS’ ROOM LIBRARY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Paul and the boys watch the helicopter land and handsome Harry 'Skip' Smith emerge. Jason Smith races to greet his father, asking the other boys if they like to ski.

scenes.heading: INT. ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICE HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: The boys peer through the office window as Paul speaks on the phone and Jason chats with his father. Jason flashes a thumbs-up to his friends as Paul announces good news about reaching most parents.

scenes.heading: INT. ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICE HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Angus pleads with Paul to try calling his mother again, but Paul explains the desk clerk said no one's answering because they're away on an excursion.

scenes.heading: INT. INFIRMARY - DAY
scenes.summary: In the infirmary, Angus watches sadly as the other boys pack their suitcases. Park and Smith say goodbye with survivor's guilt, while Kountze mockingly returns Angus's stolen photograph.
scenes.turningPointNote: Angus is truly abandoned as the last holdover, deepening his isolation

scenes.heading: EXT. QUAD - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul and Angus watch the helicopter fly away, turning grimly to each other. Paul suggests making the best of it while Angus stares at the empty horizon, percolating with hurt and rage.
scenes.turningPointNote: The central relationship between Paul and Angus begins in earnest

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN STAFF COMMON ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Paul, Mary, and Angus watch 'The Newlywed Game' together. Paul reveals he came close to marriage once but they 'came to our senses,' while discussing his preference for an ascetic, monk-like existence.

scenes.heading: INT. NURSE’S QUARTERS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In the sparse nurse's quarters, Paul in pajamas takes a deep pull from a bottle of Jim Beam, mutters to himself, and collapses heavily onto the thin mattress.

scenes.heading: INT. NURSE’S QUART ERS - LATER
scenes.summary: Angus peers at the passed-out Paul, enters quietly, and steals Paul's ring of keys and flashlight from the room.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Using the stolen keys, Angus raids the kitchen freezer and chips away at a tub of freezer-burned ice cream.

scenes.heading: INT. CHAPEL - SACRISTY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In the chapel sacristy, Angus pours sacramental wine into a chalice and drinks it down, finding it not bad and taking another gulp.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN STAFF COMMON ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Angus discovers Mary asleep on the couch in the common room with a test pattern on the television.

scenes.heading: INT. CHAPEL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In the chapel at night, Angus sits at the piano, lights a cigarette, and strikes a single key, exhaling smoke that hangs in the moonlight as the note decays.

scenes.heading: INT. CHAPEL - DAWN
scenes.summary: At dawn, Angus sits in the front pew contemplating the memorial photo of Curtis Lamb. Mary enters, removes her coat, and sits in a rear pew, exchanging nods with Angus.

scenes.heading: INT. DINING HALL - DAY
scenes.summary: At lunch, Paul proudly presents Christmas cookies as a surprise gift to share, making exaggerated yummy noises while Angus stares at him like a loser and asks to go to the bathroom.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Angus attempts to book a hotel room using a pay phone, but Paul discovers him and hangs up the phone. Their confrontation escalates as Angus calls them 'two losers and a grieving mom' and demands they stay out of each other's way.
scenes.turningPointNote: The central conflict between Paul and Angus reaches a breaking point with open hostility

scenes.heading: INT. TROPHY ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Angus dashes up stairs to trophy room with Paul struggling to keep up, both heading toward the new gymnasium

scenes.heading: INT. NEW GYMNASIUM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Angus defies Paul's warnings about the off-limits gymnasium, stepping onto the floor and attempting the pommel horse despite Paul's threats
scenes.turningPointNote: Angus crosses his Rubicon moment, defying authority and setting up the injury that follows

scenes.heading: EXT. FACULTY PARKING LOT - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul frantically scrapes ice from his car windshield while injured Angus urges him to hurry to get medical help

scenes.heading: INT. NOVA - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul drives panicked to the hospital while Angus suffers in pain, both arguing about responsibility for the accident

scenes.heading: INT. BARTON HOSPITAL - EMERGENCY ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: At the hospital, Paul fears losing his job when Angus cleverly lies to protect them both from school consequences
scenes.turningPointNote: Angus protects Paul by creating false hockey story, showing unexpected loyalty

scenes.heading: INT. BARTON HOSPITAL - TREATMENT ROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Doctor examines Angus's dislocated shoulder and explains the painful procedure needed to pop it back into place

scenes.heading: INT. BARTON HOSPITAL - PHARMACY - DAY
scenes.summary: At hospital pharmacy getting Percodan, Paul lectures Angus about Barton men not lying while Angus defends his protective deception

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WINNING TICKET - EVENING
scenes.summary: Paul and Angus arrive at a working-class tavern called The Winning Ticket for dinner

scenes.heading: INT. THE WINNING TICKET - EVENING
scenes.summary: Paul and Angus dine at the tavern where Miss Crane works as waitress, meeting local Vietnam veterans and discussing class differences
scenes.turningPointNote: Paul's compassion for working-class veterans reveals his deeper understanding of privilege and sacrifice

scenes.heading: EXT. BARTON STREET - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Outside the tavern, Paul explains to Angus the class privilege of Barton boys avoiding Vietnam unlike working-class kids

scenes.heading: EXT. BARTON STREET - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Paul struggles with his car door routine while Angus prepares to give him honest feedback about something personal

scenes.heading: INT. N OVA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Angus tells Paul he smells like fish, leading Paul to reveal his medical condition trimethylaminuria and mutual vulnerability sharing
scenes.turningPointNote: Major breakthrough in their relationship through honest, vulnerable conversation about personal struggles

scenes.heading: INT. DINING HALL - MORNING
scenes.summary: At breakfast, Mary questions why they missed dinner while Danny discovers vomit in the gymnasium, raising suspicions

scenes.heading: EXT. QUAD - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul takes a constitutional walk alone in the quad and awkwardly throws an abandoned football

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul helps Mary prepare Christmas dinner in the kitchen, finding the work therapeutic while Angus eyes brownies for the party

scenes.heading: INT. INFIRMARY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Angus practices shaving in the infirmary though he barely needs to, preparing for the Christmas party

scenes.heading: INT. MARY'S APARTMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mary looks through her closet and sees Curtis's military uniform and a mysterious brown box, noticing it's snowing again

scenes.heading: INT. NURSE’S QUARTERS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Paul gets dressed for the party and attempts to mask his body odor with air freshener

scenes.heading: EXT. NEW ENGLAND ROAD - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The three drive through town toward Miss Crane's Christmas party

scenes.heading: EXT. MISS CRAN E’S HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Paul, Mary and Angus arrive at Miss Crane's house where she greets them warmly with drink in hand

scenes.heading: INT. MISS CRANE’S HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: At the Christmas party, Paul feels overwhelmed by the crowd while Miss Crane introduces him to her family members
scenes.turningPointNote: Paul experiences normal family life and social connection, highlighting his isolation

scenes.heading: INT. MISS CRANE’S HOUSE - BASEMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elise leads Angus to the basement art area where they discuss creativity and Picasso while working on crafts together

scenes.heading: INT. MISS CRANE’S HOUSE - DEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mary manages music at the party while Danny gives her a Christmas gift, creating an awkward romantic moment

scenes.heading: INT. MISS CRANE’S HOUSE - BASEMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elise and Angus finger-paint together with flirtatious tension as she catches him trying to look down her shirt

scenes.heading: INT. MISS CRANE’S HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Paul and Miss Crane share an awkward moment under mistletoe while discussing their families and Paul's troubled past
scenes.turningPointNote: Paul opens up about his painful family history and explains why Barton feels like home

scenes.heading: INT. MISS CRANE’S HOUSE - KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mary breaks down crying in the kitchen about Curtis being gone while Danny and Paul try unsuccessfully to comfort her
scenes.turningPointNote: Mary's grief explodes, showing the real cost of the war and her devastating loss

scenes.heading: EXT. MISS CRANE’S HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Paul and Angus help devastated Mary leave the party while arguing about returning versus taking care of her
scenes.turningPointNote: Argument reveals Paul's resentment about babysitting duty and being abandoned by Angus's mother

scenes.heading: INT. NURSE’S QUARTERS - EARLY MORNING
scenes.summary: Paul lies awake in his quarters, unable to sleep after the difficult evening

scenes.heading: INT. INFIRMARY - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul checks on sleeping Angus in the infirmary the morning after the party

scenes.heading: EXT. FACULTY PARKING LOT - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul trudges through more snow to his car in the faculty parking lot

scenes.heading: EXT. ROAD - DAY
scenes.summary: The Nova drives past snowy scenery on Christmas morning

scenes.heading: EXT. CHRISTMAS TREE LOT - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul buys a small Christmas tree from the working-class guys at a tree lot having a fire sale

scenes.heading: EXT. ROAD - DAY
scenes.summary: The Nova returns to school with the small tree tied to its roof

scenes.heading: INT. INFIRMARY - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul discovers Angus missing from the infirmary and calls out searching for him

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul asks hungover Mary about Angus's whereabouts but she hasn't seen him either

scenes.heading: EXT. BA RTON ACADEMY - QUAD - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul searches the empty quad calling for Angus

scenes.heading: INT. STAIRWAY - HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul climbs stairs searching for Angus and follows piano music to find him

scenes.heading: INT. AUDITORIUM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Paul finds Angus playing piano in the auditorium and wishes him Merry Christmas

scenes.heading: INT. DINING HALL - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul proudly presents his makeshift Christmas tree with gifts underneath to Mary and Angus in the dining hall
scenes.turningPointNote: Paul creates a family Christmas moment, showing his growth toward connection and care

scenes.heading: EXT. RESIDENTIAL STREET - ROXBURY, BOSTON - DAY
scenes.summary: The Nova parks across from Mary's sister's triple-decker apartment building in Roxbury

scenes.heading: INT. NOVA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Mary insists on staying with her sister rather than a hotel, needing a break from Paul and Angus's bickering

scenes.heading: INT. PEGGY’S TOWNHOUSE - GUEST BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Mary unpacks baby clothes from her box into the nursery and shares an emotional hug with pregnant sister Peggy
scenes.turningPointNote: Mary's box reveals she lost a baby, explaining depth of her grief over Curtis

scenes.heading: INT. PEGGY’S TOWNHOUSE - GUEST BEDROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Mary and Peggy bond together on the bed, laughing and talking while Lester watches happily

scenes.heading: EXT. BOSTON STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul and Angus walk through sun-dappled, snowy Boston streets during their city adventure

scenes.heading: EXT. ANOTHER BOSTON STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: They continue walking through bustling holiday crowds on another Boston street

scenes.heading: EXT. BRATTLE BOOK SHOP - DAY
scenes.summary: At an outdoor bookstore, a prostitute approaches Paul while Angus encourages him to accept her offer

scenes.heading: INT. BOSTON FINE ARTS MUSEUM - DAY
scenes.summary: At the Museum of Fine Arts, Paul shows Angus erotic Greek pottery to teach him about unchanging human nature

scenes.heading: EXT. PARK - SKATING RINK - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Paul watches admiringly as Angus skates laps at a festive public ice rink

scenes.heading: EXT. SKATING RINK - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Leaving the rink, Paul encounters Harvard classmate Hugh Cavanaugh and lies about his current position and success
scenes.turningPointNote: Paul's humiliating encounter with successful former classmate exposes his failure and shame

scenes.heading: INT. LIQUOR STORE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: At a liquor store, Angus confronts Paul about lying to his Harvard friend, leading Paul to reveal his plagiarism scandal
scenes.turningPointNote: Paul reveals the traumatic Harvard incident that destroyed his academic career and shaped his bitterness

scenes.heading: EXT. BOSTON STREET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Paul reveals his deepest secret to Angus - he never graduated college and has been living a lie at Barton. They share a drink as Paul opens up about Dr. Green being the only one who knew and gave him a chance.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: In their hotel room, Paul discovers Angus is taking Librium for depression, though Angus tries to deflect by calling them vitamins. Paul sees through the lie but doesn't push further.

scenes.heading: INT. CANDLEPIN BOWLING ALLEY - DAY
scenes.summary: At a bowling alley, Paul and Angus bond over the game. They exchange barbs about each other's failures - Paul's poor bowling, Angus being disliked at school. Angus notices girls watching him and asks Paul which eye to look at.

scenes.heading: INT. MOVIE THEATRE BALCONY
scenes.summary: Paul and Angus watch 'Little Big Man' at a movie theater. Paul rudely corrects a patron who shushes him, while Angus excuses himself to go to the bathroom.

scenes.heading: INT. MOVIE THEATRE LOBBY - DAY
scenes.summary: Instead of going to the bathroom, Angus heads for the theater's front doors, executing a planned escape.

scenes.heading: INT. MOVIE THEATRE - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul realizes Angus has disappeared from his seat and panics, understanding he's been deceived.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOVIE THEATRE - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul catches Angus climbing into a taxi outside the theater. Despite Angus's protests that he'll return and it's nothing bad, Paul demands he get out, feeling betrayed by the deception.
scenes.turningPointNote: Paul's trust is shattered as he realizes Angus planned this escape

scenes.heading: EXT. PSYCHI ATRIC HOSPITAL DRIVEWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: The taxi drives up a long access road to a psychiatric hospital, revealing Angus's true destination.

scenes.heading: EXT. PSYCHIATRIC HOSPITAL - DAY
scenes.summary: The taxi arrives at the large stone psychiatric hospital building.

scenes.heading: INT. PSYCHIATRIC HOSPITAL CORRIDOR - DAY
scenes.summary: An orderly leads Paul and Angus through the hospital corridors. Angus enters a room alone while Paul waits outside on a bench.

scenes.heading: INT. PSYCHIATRIC HOSPITAL DAY ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Angus visits his father Thomas, who is heavily medicated and confused. The heartbreaking reunion shows Thomas doesn't fully recognize his son, revealing the tragic reason behind Angus's desperate need to see him.
scenes.turningPointNote: The devastating revelation of Angus's father's condition explains his behavior and desperation

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGHWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In a taxi heading back to Boston through falling snow, Angus gazes sadly out the window while Paul watches him with new understanding.

scenes.heading: INT. FANCY RESTAURANT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: At a fancy restaurant, Angus opens up completely to Paul about his father's mental decline, his mother's abandonment, and his own self-destructive behavior. He reveals his fear of ending up like his father and his certainty that he'll be sent to military school.
scenes.turningPointNote: Angus's complete emotional breakdown and confession creates deep intimacy between him and Paul

scenes.heading: EXT. FANCY RESTAURANT PARKING LOT - NIG HT
scenes.summary: In the restaurant parking lot, Paul attempts to make cherries jubilee for the group but uses too much alcohol, causing the takeout container to catch fire in a moment of chaotic bonding.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEW ENGLAND ROAD - DAY
scenes.summary: The Nova drives home on a New England road, the trio returning from their transformative Boston trip.

scenes.heading: INT. NOVA - DAY
scenes.summary: Inside the car, Paul, Angus, and Mary ride in contemplative silence, each wearing faint smiles that suggest their shared experience has changed them.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN STAFF COMMON ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: On New Year's Eve, the foursome watches Guy Lombardo on TV. Angus produces an M-80 firecracker, leading to playful banter about indoor fireworks as they countdown to 1971 together.

scenes.heading: EXT. KITCHEN STAFF COMMON ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Paul lights the M-80 for Angus outside, and they celebrate the new year with hugs and explosions, marking a moment of genuine family-like connection before the scene fades to black.
scenes.turningPointNote: The makeshift family's peak moment of happiness before reality returns

scenes.heading: EXT. QUAD - DAY
scenes.summary: The new semester begins on January 11, 1971, with the cross-country team racing across the quad, signaling the return to normal school life.

scenes.heading: INT. WEIGHT ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: The crew team works out on rowing machines in the weight room as their coach keeps cadence, showing the resumption of structured school activities.

scenes.heading: INT. NEW GYMNASIUM - DAY
scenes.summary: The basketball team runs drills in the new gymnasium, continuing the montage of returned normalcy.

scenes.heading: INT. STUDENT DORMITORY - DAY
scenes.summary: Students are back in the dormitories, dressing and playing. Smith emerges from the shower with freshly cut hair, showing everyone has returned changed from their breaks.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Mary is back in the kitchen managing other cooks, correcting their use of too much paprika in soup, showing her return to authority and normalcy.

scenes.heading: INT. PAUL’S CLASSROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: In Paul's classroom, students await class including a severely sunburned Kountze. Paul makes jokes about Kountze's ski vacation burns while Angus shows new confidence in defending himself.

scenes.heading: EXT. ADMINISTRATION BUILDING - DAY
scenes.summary: A well-groomed couple in a shiny Cadillac arrives at the administration building, suggesting trouble ahead.

scenes.heading: INT. PAUL’S CLASSROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Miss Crane interrupts Paul grading exams to urgently summon him to see Dr. Woodrup. Paul thanks her for the New Year's party invitation, showing his growth in social connections.

scenes.heading: INT. HEADMASTER’S OUTER OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul is surprised to find Angus waiting in the headmaster's outer office. Their eyes meet with mutual concern as Paul enters Woodrup's office, sensing serious trouble.

scenes.heading: INT. HEADMASTER’S OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Paul meets Angus's mother Judy and stepfather Stanley Clotfelter, who confront him about taking Angus to Boston. The visit to his father has caused problems, with Thomas becoming violent and expecting to come home.

scenes.heading: INT. HEADMASTER’S OUTER OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Angus waits anxiously outside, knowing he faces military school. Mary arrives and comforts him by taking his hand, showing their bond has deepened.

scenes.heading: INT. HEADMASTER’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: The Clotfelters reveal that Angus's visit upset his father Thomas, who became violent and attacked an orderly with a snow globe. They want to withdraw Angus from Barton and send him to Fork Union Military Academy.
scenes.turningPointNote: The consequences of the Boston trip threaten to destroy Angus's future

scenes.heading: INT. HEADMASTER'S OUTER OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Mary and Angus wait holding hands, hearing approaching footsteps that will determine Angus's fate.

scenes.heading: INT. HEADMASTER'S OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Paul emerges from his meeting with Woodrup, who tells him he did this to himself. Paul responds by calling the headmaster 'penis cancer in human form,' showing he's sacrificed his career for Angus.
scenes.turningPointNote: Paul's ultimate sacrifice - destroying his career to save Angus from military school

scenes.heading: EXT. HEADMASTER’S OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Paul tells Angus which eye to look at, answering his earlier question with warmth. As Paul walks away, Angus is called into the office, but Paul's sacrifice has clearly saved him.

scenes.heading: INT. FACULTY RESIDENCE - PAUL’S ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul packs his belongings in his emptied faculty room. Mary visits and gives him a leather notebook for his monograph, showing their deep friendship and her belief in his worth.

scenes.heading: EXT. REAR OF FACULTY RESIDENCE - DAY
scenes.summary: Students watch Paul pack his U-Haul trailer. Kountze spreads crude rumors about why Paul was fired, while Angus watches silently, knowing the truth about Paul's sacrifice.

scenes.heading: EXT. FACULTY PARKING LOT - DAY
scenes.summary: In their final goodbye, Angus thanks Paul and suggests skipping PE to get ice cream together. Paul gently refuses, encouraging Angus to go to class and move forward with his life at Barton.
scenes.turningPointNote: The final goodbye between Paul and Angus, with Paul encouraging him to embrace his future

scenes.heading: EXT. BARTON CAMPUS - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul drives past the Main Hall in his Nova, towing the U-Haul trailer, leaving Barton Academy behind.

scenes.heading: INT. NOVA - DAY
scenes.summary: Through the windshield, the gothic buildings recede as Paul approaches the back gate. His face reveals both terror and hope at leaving the only home he's ever known.

scenes.heading: EXT. BARTON DRIVEWAY / FRONT GATE - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul stops at the edge of the road at Barton's front gate, pausing at the threshold between his old life and uncertain future.

scenes.heading: INT. NOVA - DAY
scenes.summary: Paul takes a swig of Woodrup's expensive Louis XIII cognac, swishes it like mouthwash and spits it out in a final act of defiance. He drives away into the distance, leaving Barton forever but moving toward an unknown future with dignity.
scenes.turningPointNote: Paul's final rejection of the institution and embrace of an uncertain but honest future
